summary:
RM spoke with Mr. James Hargrove (President & CEO, Hargrove Construction Inc.) to formally initiate credit assessment for a combined facility of $72–85M ($40–50M term loan capex, $18–20M equipment financing, $15M revolving line of credit). Austin, TX site lease signed — 12 acres. Existing banking: Wells Fargo + JPMorgan Chase ($42M total, clean). Disbursement deadline: June 2026.

Cross-sell confirmed: Borrower explicitly requested a structured FX hedging facility (EUR/JPY, $8–10M p.a.) and expressed clear interest in Cash Management Services for Federal billing cycle optimisation.

Next action agreed: Credit meeting Friday March 28, 11 AM — RM + CFO Mr. David Walsh. Document checklist to be sent same day. Recommend expedited credit evaluation with FX hedging and CMS proposals included in term sheet package.

transcript.speaker: RM
transcript.text: Good afternoon Mr. Hargrove, Michael Chen here from the Commercial Banking team. Thanks for your time today. I wanted to follow up on the Austin facility expansion and formally kick off the credit assessment.

transcript.speaker: Borrower
transcript.text: Good afternoon. Perfect timing — we just executed the ground lease yesterday. 12 acres in Austin, TX. We're ready to move forward.

transcript.speaker: RM
transcript.text: Excellent. Walk me through the complete financing requirement please.

transcript.speaker: Borrower
transcript.text: Civil construction and site development is $40–50M. Equipment procurement for the precast machinery is another $18–20M. We'll need a revolving line of credit of $15M to manage the ramp-up period. All in, we're looking at roughly $72–85M.

transcript.speaker: RM
transcript.text: Understood. And your existing banking — are you with a single lender or multiple?

transcript.speaker: Borrower
transcript.text: Multiple banks. Wells Fargo holds our revolving line of credit at $22M, and JPMorgan Chase has a term loan of $20M maturing next year. Total existing exposure is about $42M. We have a clean repayment record on both.

transcript.speaker: RM
transcript.text: Good to hear. Can you give me a brief background on the company's founding and your leadership history?

transcript.speaker: Borrower
transcript.text: My father started the company in 1987 as a general contractor. We moved into full infrastructure and heavy civil by 2004. I came on board in 2012 — MBA from Wharton. We've completed 60+ infrastructure projects across Texas and Arizona. It's a family-owned enterprise — no other business interests.

transcript.speaker: RM
transcript.text: Strong track record. What are your capital plans beyond the Austin facility?

transcript.speaker: Borrower
transcript.text: Austin is Phase 1 — target commissioning Q3 2027. Phase 2 is a second precast facility in Phoenix, Arizona in 24–30 months. We're also bidding on FHWA highway projects — that could require $25–30M additional equipment if we win. We're projecting revenues of $600M by 2029.

transcript.speaker: RM
transcript.text: That's strong growth. Any foreign currency exposure we should know about?

transcript.speaker: Borrower
transcript.text: Yes — we import specialized machinery components from Germany and Japan, around $8–10M annually in EUR and JPY. We've been hedging on an ad-hoc basis through JPMorgan. We'd really welcome a structured FX hedging program — it's something we've been wanting to formalize.

transcript.speaker: RM
transcript.text: Absolutely — our Treasury desk can build that out. Also, given your Federal contract receivables, have you considered a formal Cash Management Services arrangement? It would centralize collections and give you much better working capital visibility.

transcript.speaker: Borrower
transcript.text: That's something we've actually been looking for. Yes, please include CMS in the proposal. Our Federal billing cycle runs about 45 days — centralised collections would really help us manage cash flow.

transcript.speaker: RM
transcript.text: How does your revenue model work — direct contracts or through a subcontracting network?

transcript.speaker: Borrower
transcript.text: Entirely direct-to-client — no subcontractor or dealer network. About 65% Federal and Municipal — FHWA, TxDOT, Army Corps of Engineers — and 35% private developers. Milestone-based billing on all contracts.

transcript.speaker: RM
transcript.text: Any affiliated entities or subsidiaries we should include in the credit package?

transcript.speaker: Borrower
transcript.text: Yes — Hargrove Holdings LLC is the promoter holding entity. And we have HG-Constructors, a 51% JV set up for a Phoenix mixed-use development. That's ring-fenced — no cross-default exposure. No other affiliates.

transcript.speaker: RM
transcript.text: Perfect. On next steps — I'll send you a formal document checklist by end of today. Can we set up a credit meeting with my team and your CFO this Friday at 11 AM?

transcript.speaker: Borrower
transcript.text: Friday works great. Our CFO David Walsh will be on that call too. We're keen to move quickly given our June 2026 disbursement deadline.

transcript.speaker: RM
transcript.text: Confirmed. I'll send the calendar invite and document checklist right away. Thank you Mr. Hargrove — talk Friday.
